mechanism.summary: Design: a "declared server aggregate" pipeline that generalizes the existing P1-T5 primitive (public.fillout_cohort_stats in apps/server/migrations/00028_cohort_stats.sql + public_cohort_stats in apps/server/src/api/sessions/analytics.rs). Feedback widgets bind to named aggregate declarations stored in the questionnaire definition (settings.serverAggregates); a compile step deduplicates them per definition version. A new public batch endpoint GET /api/questionnaires/{id}/feedback-aggregates resolves the declarations SERVER-SIDE from the stored definition JSONB (the client never sends a filter — only the questionnaire id + version), computes each via a generalized SECURITY DEFINER function with dataset-filter support, and applies the existing MIN_COHORT_N=5 floor per aggregate. The client fetches this batch at the same moment it caches the definition (routes/(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.ts online branch, right after FilloutContentCache.cacheQuestionnaire) and stores rows in a new Dexie table filloutAggregates keyed [definitionKey+aggregateId] (definitionKey = filloutDefinitionKey(questionnaireId, maj, min, patch), matching the version-pinning discipline of filloutQuestionnaires). During fillout — fully offline — the statistical-feedback engine gains a 'cohort-synced' source mode that resolves the widget's aggregateId against an in-memory snapshot of those Dexie rows loaded once at page init: a pure lookup, zero network. Fallback is layered: synced row → optional bundled norm table (E-FEEDBACK-2 path) → honest "group comparison unavailable offline" state; n<5 rows carry the count with null stats and render "not enough responses yet". This makes 'bundled norm = literature, synced aggregate = this study's own cohort' a first-class, offline-correct distinction.
mechanism.declaration_config: Two-level declaration, both living inside the questionnaire definition JSONB (so they version with the questionnaire and travel inside the pinned snapshot the offline runtime already loads). (1) Questionnaire-level registry, new types in packages/questionnaire-core, stored at definition.settings.serverAggregates: interface ServerAggregateDecl { id: string; source: 'variable'|'response'; key: string; stats: Array<'n'|'mean'|'sd'|'min'|'max'|'median'|'p90'|'p95'|'p99'>; dataset?: DatasetFilter } and interface DatasetFilter { id: string; label?: string; versionScope?: 'any'|'sameMajor'|'exact'; completedAfter?: string; completedBefore?: string; where?: Array<{ var: string; op: 'eq'|'ne'|'lt'|'lte'|'gt'|'gte'|'in'; value: string|number|Array<string|number> }> } (where-clauses restricted to session variables in v1 — session_variable_index from 00012 makes them cheap). (2) Widget-level binding by id, extending StatisticalFeedbackDataSourceConfig (apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts) with aggregateId?: string plus new sourceMode 'cohort-synced'. Concrete example — box chart of the participant's anxiety score vs cohort mean±SD over female participants only: settings.serverAggregates = [{ "id": "agg_anxiety_f", "source": "variable", "key": "score.anxiety.value", "stats": ["n","mean","sd","min","max","median"], "dataset": { "id": "females", "label": "Female participants", "versionScope": "sameMajor", "where": [{ "var": "demo.gender", "op": "eq", "value": "f" }] } }]; widget config = { "sourceMode": "cohort-synced", "chartType": "box", "dataSource": { "currentVariable": "score.anxiety.value", "aggregateId": "agg_anxiety_f", "fallbackNormTableId": "phq9-general-population" } }. The designer UI (StatisticalFeedbackDesigner.svelte) creates/edits declarations and offers existing ones for reuse; a pure compile helper collectServerAggregates(definition) (new file apps/web/src/lib/runtime/feedback/serverAggregates.ts) returns the deduplicated declaration list — used by the designer for validation and by the sync fetcher to know what to expect. Cap the registry (e.g. 50 declarations) at validation time.
mechanism.server_compute: New migration apps/server/migrations/00030_feedback_aggregates.sql: CREATE FUNCTION public.fillout_feedback_stats(qid uuid, p_source text, p_key text, p_filter jsonb) RETURNS TABLE(n bigint, mean float8, std_dev float8, min float8, max float8, median float8, p90 float8, p95 float8, p99 float8) — same LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_temp posture, OWNER qdesigner, REVOKE FROM PUBLIC + GRANT EXECUTE TO qdesigner_app as 00028. Body reuses the 00028 vals CTE and JSONB numeric coercion verbatim, adding filter predicates on sessions: version scope (AND (p_filter->>'versionScope' IS DISTINCT FROM 'sameMajor' OR s.questionnaire_version_major = (p_filter->>'versionMajor')::int) — analogous for 'exact'), date bounds (AND (p_filter->>'completedAfter' IS NULL OR s.completed_at >= (p_filter->>'completedAfter')::timestamptz), same for before), and per where-clause an EXISTS subquery: AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM session_variable_index svi2 WHERE svi2.session_id = s.id AND svi2.variable_name = w->>'var' AND <op applied to svi2.numeric_value or raw_value text>) iterated over jsonb_array_elements(p_filter->'where'). 00028's fillout_cohort_stats stays untouched (back-compat for the existing 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' live modes). New handler public_feedback_aggregates in apps/server/src/api/sessions/analytics.rs, route .route("/{id}/feedback-aggregates", get(sessions::public_feedback_aggregates)) next to the existing cohort-stats route in apps/server/src/api/mod.rs (line ~180). Flow: (1) published-questionnaire gate exactly as public_cohort_stats (status='published' AND deleted_at IS NULL, 404 otherwise, no existence probing); (2) load the definition JSONB — from questionnaire_definitions for the latest, or from questionnaire_snapshots (00010) when an optional ?version=maj.min.patch query targets a pinned older version; (3) read definition->'settings'->'serverAggregates' — THIS is the authorization model: the server only ever evaluates filters the designer published; the anonymous client sends zero filter data; (4) for each declaration (capped, e.g. 50) run fillout_feedback_stats via sqlx on &state.pool (no RLS GUC needed — definer bypasses, as documented in analytics.rs); (5) apply MIN_COHORT_N (models.rs line 1361) per aggregate: n<5 → sample_count populated, every stat None; (6) respond { questionnaire_id, version, computed_at, aggregates: [{ id, source, key, dataset_id, stats: NumericStatsSummary }] }. Cost bound: wrap step 4–6 in a Redis TTL cache (state.redis, key feedagg:{qid}:{version}, TTL ~300s) so anonymous load spikes don't fan out into N aggregate scans per page view.
mechanism.sync_flow: WHEN: (a) primary fetch point is the online branch of routes/(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.ts, immediately after await FilloutContentCache.cacheQuestionnaire(payload, code) (line ~64): call a new FilloutContentCache.cacheAggregates(payload) that GETs /api/questionnaires/{id}/feedback-aggregates?version=... and writes rows — awaited with a short timeout (~3s) so the first-ever visit lands values before the participant can reach the report page, but a failure never blocks load (catch → proceed; staleness handling covers it); (b) when the load resolves a PINNED older version for a resume (the `pin` branch, +page.ts line ~180), fetch with ?version=pin so declarations from the pinned snapshot get fresh values too; (c) opportunistic refresh piggybacked on FilloutUploadSync.syncNow() (apps/web/src/lib/fillout/services/FilloutUploadSync.ts) after a successful drain — this also means a cohort that just absorbed this participant's own completed session refreshes promptly for the next run on the same device. WHERE: new Dexie table in apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts, schema version(5) — filloutAggregates: '[definitionKey+aggregateId], definitionKey, questionnaireId, syncedAt'. Row shape: { definitionKey /* filloutDefinitionKey(questionnaireId, maj, min, patch) — same composite the definition snapshot uses */, aggregateId, questionnaireId, source, key, datasetId, n, stats: { mean, stdDev, min, max, median, p90, p95, p99 } | null, computedAt /* server timestamp */, syncedAt /* client clock */ }. Keying by definitionKey keeps aggregate rows aligned with the version-pinned definition rows: a background refresh to a newer version writes new rows and never clobbers the set a pinned in-flight session reads; FilloutContentCache.pruneDefinitions() gains one line to delete filloutAggregates rows whose definitionKey it just GC'd. STALENESS: each row carries syncedAt; the page-load fetch refetches when the newest row for the definitionKey is older than a designer-configurable settings.serverAggregates refreshMs (default 24h) or missing. Offline, staleness never blocks: render from whatever exists and surface computedAt to the widget so it can caption "cohort data as of <date>" (and optionally a stronger stale warning past staleAfterMs).
mechanism.offline_render: At fillout page init ((fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte, where the runtime is constructed), after the definitionKey is known, do ONE Dexie read: db.filloutAggregates.where('definitionKey').equals(key).toArray() → an in-memory Record<aggregateId, SyncedAggregate> passed into the runtime context alongside variables (mirroring how urlParams/variables already flow). From that moment rendering is synchronous and network-free. In apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts, resolveStatisticalFeedbackSeries gains a 'cohort-synced' branch (sibling of the existing fully-local 'norm-table' branch at line ~384): participant value = resolveNumericValue(dataSource.currentVariable, variables, metric) — this reads the E-FEEDBACK-1 ScaleScorer output ('score.<id>' objects, already navigable via the existing resolveVariableValue dotted-path walker at line ~128); cohort = syncedAggregates[dataSource.aggregateId]. It then reuses sessionAnalyticsService.buildParticipantVsCohortSeriesLocal (apps/web/src/lib/services/sessionAnalytics.ts line 234) with the cached stats mapped into SessionAggregateData — same z-score math, same ChartSeriesContract, so every existing chart (BoxPlot/bar/radar/bell-curve in modules/display/statistical-feedback/charts/) renders unchanged. Box-chart example: box = mean±sd from the cached row, median line = stats.median, whiskers = min/max (or p90/p95), participant marker = score.anxiety.value from the VariableEngine; caption "n=142, as of 2026-07-04". The exact read path is: Dexie filloutAggregates ([definitionKey+aggregateId]) → page-init snapshot Record → engine branch → buildParticipantVsCohortSeriesLocal → chart component. No fetch() exists anywhere on this path — unlike the current 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' modes which call sessionAnalyticsService.aggregatePublic live (engine.ts line ~485) and therefore break offline.
mechanism.fallback_behavior: Layered, and always honest (the F060 lesson: never render a silent null 'No data' point). (1) Cached row present, n>=5: normal render with "as of computedAt" caption. (2) Cached row present but n<5 (server withheld stats, kept count — MIN_COHORT_N contract from analytics.rs): render the participant-only view plus "Group comparison needs at least 5 completed responses (currently n)". (3) No cached row at all — pure-offline first run (definition arrived via another channel or aggregates fetch failed and the device went offline): if the widget declares fallbackNormTableId, degrade to the existing E-FEEDBACK-2 norm-table path (getNormTable / CUSTOM_NORM_TABLE_ID in runtime/feedback/normTables.ts) with an explicit "compared against published norms, not this study's participants" label — the designer opted into this semantic substitution; otherwise render the participant's own score with a "Group comparison unavailable offline — reconnect to see how you compare" state. (4) Stale row (older than staleAfterMs, e.g. 30 days): still render (offline correctness beats freshness) but with a visible staleness note; the next online page load refreshes it. (5) Version-pin miss (session pinned a version whose snapshot this device lacks — the pinnedFallback branch in +page.ts line ~193): aggregate rows keyed to the latest definitionKey are used as best-effort only when the aggregateId exists in the effective definition; otherwise case (3) applies. Designer preview (renderMode 'edit'/'preview' per FeedbackRenderMode) bypasses the cache and hits the endpoint live so researchers see current values while designing.
data_model_changes: packages/questionnaire-core: add ServerAggregateDecl + DatasetFilter types; questionnaire settings gains serverAggregates: ServerAggregateDecl[] (versioned inside the definition JSONB — no relational schema change)

apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts: StatisticalSourceMode gains 'cohort-synced'; StatisticalFeedbackDataSourceConfig gains aggregateId?: string and fallbackNormTableId?: string; validateStatisticalFeedbackConfig gains a cohort-synced branch (requires aggregateId + currentVariable)

apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts: Dexie version(5) adds filloutAggregates: '[definitionKey+aggregateId], definitionKey, questionnaireId, syncedAt' with row {definitionKey, aggregateId, questionnaireId, source, key, datasetId, n, stats|null, computedAt, syncedAt}; purely additive, all v4 stores carried over

apps/web/src/lib/fillout/services/FilloutContentCache.ts: new cacheAggregates(payload, version?) fetcher + one-line extension of pruneDefinitions() to GC filloutAggregates rows for deleted definitionKeys
apps/web/src/lib/runtime/feedback/serverAggregates.ts (new): collectServerAggregates(definition) compile/dedupe helper + SyncedAggregate type shared by cache, page init, and engine
apps/server/src/api/sessions/models.rs: FeedbackAggregatesResponse / FeedbackAggregateEntry (utoipa-annotated); reuse NumericStatsSummary and MIN_COHORT_N
apps/server/src/api/sessions/analytics.rs: public_feedback_aggregates handler; apps/server/src/api/mod.rs: route '/{id}/feedback-aggregates' beside line 180's cohort-stats route

apps/server/migrations/00030_feedback_aggregates.sql: public.fillout_feedback_stats(qid, source, key, filter jsonb) SECURITY DEFINER function (00028 generalized with filter predicates); 00028's function untouched
mechanism.migration_needed: Yes, two, both additive and zero-risk to existing data. (1) SQL: 00030_feedback_aggregates.sql creates only the new SECURITY DEFINER function (owner qdesigner, EXECUTE granted to qdesigner_app, REVOKE FROM PUBLIC) — no table/RLS changes, no backfill; fillout_cohort_stats from 00028 remains for the live 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' modes. (2) Dexie: version(5) in indexeddb.ts adding the filloutAggregates store — Dexie creates it empty, no .upgrade() body needed, all existing stores restated unchanged (same pattern as the v3→v4 bump). Existing questionnaires need nothing: definitions without settings.serverAggregates simply yield an empty aggregates response, and no widget references a 'cohort-synced' mode until a designer configures one.
mechanism.security_privacy: Four guarantees, all inherited or tightened from the P1-T5 posture. (1) Anonymity floor: MIN_COHORT_N = 5 (apps/server/src/api/sessions/models.rs:1361) enforced in the handler PER aggregate PER dataset — a filtered cohort (e.g. gender=f AND completed this month) that shrinks below 5 returns count-only with null stats, exactly like public_cohort_stats today. (2) No per-session leakage: only the generalized SECURITY DEFINER function crosses the RLS boundary and it returns aggregates exclusively (count/moments/percentiles), same containment argument documented in 00028_cohort_stats.sql lines 11–20; the client-side cache therefore only ever holds population statistics — no other participant's data lands on a device. (3) Dataset-filter authorization: filters are NOT caller-supplied. The anonymous client sends only questionnaire id (+ version); the server resolves filter specs from the PUBLISHED definition JSONB it already stores. An attacker cannot probe with crafted filters (the classic differencing attack of narrowing a filter until n=6 vs 5 isolates one person) because the only computable filters are the finite designer-declared set; the published-only 404 gate from public_cohort_stats is retained so unpublished questionnaires expose nothing. (4) Cost/abuse bounds: declaration cap (~50), Redis TTL result cache, and the existing rate-limit middleware on the public router. Residual risk to document: differencing ACROSS designer-declared aggregates (e.g. 'all' minus 'female' reveals a small male subgroup) is possible by construction — designer-facing, mitigated by a designer-time lint warning and optionally enforcing the floor on declared-filter complements (open question).
open_questions: Version-scoped declaration resolution: for a pinned older session, should ?version resolve declarations from questionnaire_snapshots (00010) — correct but requires snapshot retention — or fall back to the latest published definition's registry when the snapshot is gone (aggregateIds may have changed meaning)?
Differencing across declared aggregates: enforce the n>=5 floor on pairwise complements of declared datasets server-side (stronger, more complex), or ship a designer-time lint warning only?

First-load latency budget: is the awaited ~3s aggregates fetch in +page.ts acceptable, or should it be fully fire-and-forget with the report page tolerating a mid-session cache arrival (rows readable by completion time in practice)?

where-clause scope v1: restrict DatasetFilter.where to session variables only (session_variable_index makes it indexed and cheap) — is filtering on raw responses.value needed for launch, given its JSONB coercion cost inside an EXISTS per session?
Should FilloutUploadSync's post-drain refresh be default-on (fresher cohorts, more anonymous traffic) or gated behind settings.serverAggregates.refreshOnSync?

Server-side Redis TTL (proposed 300s) vs a materialized feedback_aggregate_cache table refreshed on session completion — at what cohort size does per-request computation of ~50 aggregates over session_variable_index become the bottleneck?

Does the designable Report Page (E-FEEDBACK-3 proper) bind widgets through this same aggregateId registry, or does the analytics dashboard-builder model (analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts) impose a different binding shape the registry should anticipate?

reportPage.summary: The Report Page is a new participant-facing composition layer, `settings.report: ReportPageConfig`, persisted inside the questionnaire definition JSON exactly like `settings.scoring` (packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts:272-305, "no DB migration" precedent) so it version-pins and ships offline inside the Dexie `filloutQuestionnaires` snapshot. It composes typed widgets on a 12-column grid whose layout model copies `analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts` (DashboardWidget/WidgetPosition), but whose rendering reuses the statistical-feedback module's already-offline-aware primitives: `resolveStatisticalFeedbackSeries` in modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts, its charts (FeedbackChart/BellCurveChart/GaugeChart), ScoreInterpreter band logic, and ReportGenerator for PDF. Each widget binds to a participant value (`score.<scaleId>` fields written by `QuestionnaireRuntime.applyScaleScores`, runtime/core/QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1565-1582, or any VariableEngine variable) plus an optional comparison source; a new `'cohort-synced'` source mode joins the engine's existing `norm-table`/`self-baseline` modes and reads the last-synced server aggregate from a new Dexie `filloutAggregates` table instead of calling `sessionAnalyticsService.aggregatePublic` live (which is what today's `'cohort'` runtime path does — and which fails offline). Server aggregates are declared first-class in `settings.report.aggregates: DeclaredAggregate[]` ({id, source, key, stats[], dataset}), computed by a generalization of `public.fillout_cohort_stats` (migration 00028) behind a batch endpoint that keeps the published-questionnaire gate and the n<5 null-stats floor of `public_cohort_stats` (apps/server/src/api/sessions/analytics.rs:584), and refreshed opportunistically by `FilloutContentCache` during the online load in `(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.ts`. At completion, the fillout page finally passes `completedSession.variables` (available today — see FilloutRuntime.handleComplete, fillout/runtime/FilloutRuntime.ts:326-333 — but currently dropped at the CompletionScreen mount, +page.svelte:889-896) into CompletionScreen, which mounts a new `ReportPageView` grid renderer above the completion-code block; the render path touches only local state (variables, bundled normTables, Dexie aggregates) — zero network. The in-flow statistical-feedback display item remains for mid-survey feedback and gains the same `'cohort-synced'` mode, so both surfaces become offline-correct through one engine.
widget_types: score-tile — headline value + band badge + T/percentile chips, driven by score.<scaleId> {value,tScore,percentile,band} from ScaleScorer (ScaleScoreResult, runtime/feedback/ScaleScorer.ts:24-40) and ScoreInterpreter ranges
bar — participant vs comparison bars; thin wrapper over FeedbackChart chartType 'bar' fed by resolveStatisticalFeedbackSeries points ([You, norm.mean] / [Participant, cohortMean])

box-cohort — box/whisker of the synced cohort distribution with the participant's score as a marker; consumes DeclaredAggregate stats {min, p25?, median, p75?, max, mean, stdDev, n}; FeedbackChart already accepts chartType 'box' + cohortMean/cohortStdDev (StatisticalFeedback.svelte:298-305) — needs p25/p75 added to fillout_cohort_stats (open question 1)
radar-profile — multi-scale profile over several score.<id> bindings, optional cohort-mean overlay series (one declared aggregate per scale); FeedbackChart 'radar' exists
distribution-with-marker — BellCurveChart.svelte (participantValue + mean + stdDev + colorRules) fed by either a bundled/custom norm or a cohort-synced aggregate
gauge — GaugeChart.svelte with min/max/color derived from interpretation ranges (existing gaugeRange derivation in StatisticalFeedback.svelte:106-119)
trajectory-line — baseline→current (or multi-administration) line reusing the engine's self-baseline mode (points [Baseline, Current], delta, reliableChangeIndex from normTables.ts)

interpretive-text — rich text with {{score.x.tScore}}-style interpolation resolved via engine.ts resolveVariableValue (handles namespaced flat keys, engine.ts:104-149) plus per-band conditional variants reusing ScoreInterpretationRange + interpretBandMessage
results-table — one row per scale: raw value, itemsAnswered/itemsExpected, z/T/percentile/band straight from ScaleScoreResult; the tabular sibling of the PDF report body
completion-meta — optional duration/answered/completion-code tile, lifting what CompletionScreen already computes (lines 46-60)
reportPage.reuse_decision: Hybrid: NEW ReportPageConfig composer type + runtime view, REUSING the statistical-feedback module's data/render layer, and copying (not importing) the analytics dashboard grid model. Rationale from the real code: (1) The analytics DashboardBuilder is the right INTERACTION pattern but the wrong stack — its WidgetType union is researcher analytics ('descriptive-stats'|'histogram'|'time-series'|'completion-funnel'|'reliability'|'irt', analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts:6-12), its widgets fetch via authenticated APIs with no offline path, and DashboardLayout lives outside the questionnaire definition so it neither version-pins nor ships in the offline snapshot. DashboardBuilder.svelte itself is a 167-line presentational grid with a renderWidget snippet prop — worth copying the DashboardWidget/WidgetPosition shapes and the WidgetContainer/WidgetPalette pattern into questionnaire-core types (parameterize the palette; WIDGET_PALETTE is hardcoded to analytics types), not worth coupling the participant runtime to lib/analytics researcher components. (2) The statistical-feedback module is the right DATA layer — engine.ts already solves per-widget offline source resolution (current-session, norm-table with CUSTOM_NORM_TABLE_ID, self-baseline with RCI), the runtime/edit/preview FeedbackRenderMode split, anonymous-safe cohort access via aggregatePublic, chart types including box/bell-curve/radar/gauge, ScoreInterpreter bands, and ReportGenerator PDF. But it is a single-item module: one chart + one binding per in-flow display item, presented through FormQuestionHost with auto-advance semantics (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1123-1169) — not a composable multi-widget page and not mountable on CompletionScreen, which sits outside the runtime item loop. (3) Extending the statistical-feedback module alone (e.g. a mega-config with N charts) would bloat a display item and still not solve designer-level page layout or CompletionScreen mounting. So: new `ReportPageView.svelte` + `ReportPageEditor.svelte` whose widgets are thin bindings onto engine.ts + the charts/ directory, with the new 'cohort-synced' StatisticalSourceMode added to engine.ts so both the report page and the existing in-flow item benefit.
reportPage.designer_composition: A new "Report" panel in the designer (sibling of the existing settings editors — ScaleScoringEditor lives at lib/components/designer/scoring/, QuotaPanel/DataQualityPanel/ExperimentalDesignPanel at lib/components/designer/), hosting `ReportPageEditor.svelte`: the DashboardBuilder pattern (Add Widget palette sidebar, grid with resize/remove via WidgetContainer) instantiated over a report-specific palette. Per-widget config popovers reuse the binding UX already built in StatisticalFeedbackDesigner.svelte: a variable/score picker that offers `score.<scaleId>` entries derived from `settings.scoring.scales` plus VariableManager variables, metric field selector (value/tScore/percentile — the fields metricValueFromObject reads), norm-table dropdown incl. CUSTOM_NORM_TABLE_ID with inline mean/sd, and interpretation-range editors (ScoreInterpreterConfig). Comparison binding: choosing source 'norm-table'/'custom-norm' is fully local; choosing 'cohort-synced' makes the editor create-or-link a DeclaredAggregate in `settings.report.aggregates` — the editor derives the aggregate from the widget's binding ((source:'variable'|'response', key, requested stats, dataset filter) — score.<id> keys work because scores are persisted as session_variables and fillout_cohort_stats already aggregates source='variable' by key), dedupes identical declarations across widgets via `aggregateRef`, and garbage-collects unreferenced ones on save. Persistence: everything under `settings.report`, so it rides the existing settings persistence, Yjs collaboration, and semver flow (adding/changing report widgets = minor bump per the versioning table; the layout is versioned with the definition). Live preview uses the same ReportPageView with mode 'preview' (authenticated), where cohort-synced widgets may fetch fresh aggregates directly to show real numbers, mirroring engine.ts's existing edit/preview vs runtime split (engine.ts:358 FeedbackRenderMode).
reportPage.completion_screen_wiring: Two dead wires get fixed in (fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte at the mount site (lines 889-896, which today pass only session/customMessage/distributionSettings/urlParams). First, variables: `completedSession.variables` is populated — QuestionnaireRuntime.complete() runs applyScaleScores() (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1635-1636) and FilloutRuntime.handleComplete persists each {variableId, value} via OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable (FilloutRuntime.ts:326-333) — so the page builds `const completedVariables = Object.fromEntries(completedSession.variables.map(v => [v.variableId, v.value]))` and passes `variables={completedVariables}`. Second, scoreConfigs: sourced from `settings.report` (each widget's interpretation config, plus legacy fallback to the union of statistical-feedback items' `config.scoreInterpretation`) so CompletionScreen's existing `showReportButton`/`handleDownloadReport` PDF path (CompletionScreen.svelte:140-158) comes alive with the same data. Third, the new surface: CompletionScreen gains `reportConfig?: ReportPageConfig` (from `rawDefinition.settings?.report` — same read pattern as `rawDefinition.settings?.distribution` at line 892, localized through the ADR 0022 resolveText path like completionMessage) and, when enabled, renders `<ReportPageView {reportConfig} variables={completedVariables} questionnaireId sessionId mode="runtime" />` between the thank-you header and the completion-code block. ReportPageView is plain Svelte inside CompletionScreen — it does NOT go through FormQuestionHost/moduleConfigAdapter because the completion screen sits outside the runtime item loop (currentScreen === 'complete' replaces the canvas+overlay entirely). Relation to the in-flow statistical-feedback display item: that item stays for mid-survey feedback (presented via formHost.present with the F062 no-auto-dismiss rule, QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1162-1169); the report page reuses its engine and charts, and both consume the new cohort-synced mode — the item for during-survey, the report for end-of-survey. The PDF button renders the same composed report via ReportGenerator (which StatisticalFeedback.svelte already feeds normativeStats, lines 181-223 — extended to accept synced aggregates).
reportPage.offline_notes: Every render input is local at completion time: (1) participant scores — ScaleScorer is pure/deterministic ("runs identically online and offline", ScaleScorer.ts:143-150) and its score.<id> outputs live in the VariableEngine and in `completedSession.variables`, persisted to Dexie filloutVariables; (2) norms — bundled statically in runtime/feedback/normTables.ts (E-FEEDBACK-2, zero network); (3) cohort aggregates — the new Dexie table `filloutAggregates` keyed `[questionnaireId+aggregateId]` holding {stats: {n,mean,stdDev,min,max,median,p90,p95,p99,(p25,p75)}, computedAt, syncedAt}, refreshed by FilloutContentCache whenever the +page.ts online-first load succeeds (right where cacheQuestionnaire/pruneDefinitions already run, +page.ts:61-66) via one batch call that posts the definition's DeclaredAggregate list; non-blocking, stale-while-revalidate. The engine's 'cohort-synced' branch reads Dexie only — unlike today's 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' runtime branches which await aggregatePublic live (engine.ts:481-535) and would throw offline. Freshness is honest UI: widgets show "cohort of n=X, synced <syncedAt>". Interplay with the sync mechanism: aggregates are deliberately NOT part of the version-pinned definition snapshot — they evolve as data accrues, so they key by questionnaireId (not filloutDefinitionKey) and refresh independently of the pinned definition; the DeclaredAggregate DECLARATIONS are versioned (in settings.report), the VALUES are not. First-visit coverage: a session can only start from an online-first load or a previously cached definition, and both paths seed/refresh aggregates, so a completed-offline session always has the last-synced values; if a declared aggregate has never synced (or the server returned null stats under the n<5 floor), the widget applies its designer-chosen fallback ('hide' | 'norm-table' | message like "not enough participants yet"), degrading to participant-only rendering. The n>=5 anonymity floor is enforced server-side per aggregate (post-dataset-filter), mirroring public_cohort_stats. Nothing in CompletionScreen/ReportPageView awaits fetch; FilloutUploadSync's syncNow() at onComplete (uploading, not downloading) is unchanged.
data_model_changes: questionnaire-core (packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts): add `report?: ReportPageConfig` to QuestionnaireSettings — { enabled, title?, layout: {columns:12, rowHeight, gap}, widgets: ReportWidget[], aggregates: DeclaredAggregate[], enablePdfDownload? }. Definition-JSON only, no DB migration (same precedent as settings.scoring/settings.translations).

ReportWidget: { id, type: ReportWidgetType, position: WidgetPosition (shape copied from analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts:14-23), title?, binding: { source: 'variable'|'score', key: string, field?: 'value'|'tScore'|'percentile'|'z'|'stanine' }, comparison?: { source: 'none'|'norm-table'|'custom-norm'|'self-baseline'|'cohort-synced', normTableId?, customNorm?: CustomNormConfig, baselineVariable?, aggregateRef?: string, fallback: 'hide'|'norm-table'|'message' }, interpretation?: ScoreInterpreterConfig, text?: { template: string, bandVariants?: Record<string,string> } }.

DeclaredAggregate: { id, source: 'variable'|'response', key, stats: AggregateStat[], dataset?: { versionScope?: 'all'|'exact'|'same-major', dateRange?, conditionGroup? } } — the widget-side consumption contract for the mechanism architect's synced-aggregate work; generalizes the (source,key) signature of public.fillout_cohort_stats (migration 00028_cohort_stats.sql:37).

Dexie (apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts): schema v4 adding `filloutAggregates: '[questionnaireId+aggregateId], questionnaireId, syncedAt'` rows { questionnaireId, aggregateId, declHash, stats: {n, mean, stdDev, min, max, median, p90, p95, p99, p25?, p75?}, computedAt, syncedAt } — additive table, safe under Dexie 4's primary-key-change constraint noted at v3.

Server: extend fillout_cohort_stats (or add fillout_declared_aggregates) with p25/p75 and dataset-filter args, still SECURITY DEFINER owned by qdesigner with EXECUTE granted to qdesigner_app (00028 lines 148-150); new public batch endpoint GET/POST /api/questionnaires/{id}/report-aggregates that resolves the published-gate once and evaluates each declaration with the per-aggregate n>=5 null-stats floor (pattern of public_cohort_stats, analytics.rs:584-624).

Engine (modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts): add 'cohort-synced' to StatisticalSourceMode + `aggregateRef` to StatisticalFeedbackDataSourceConfig; the branch reads filloutAggregates via a small AggregateStore service and emits the same ChartSeriesContract shape ({points, distribution:{mean,stdDev,n}, summary}) the charts already consume.

New components: lib/fillout/components/ReportPageView.svelte (runtime grid renderer, mounted by CompletionScreen), lib/components/designer/report/ReportPageEditor.svelte (+ generalized WidgetPalette items), thin widget wrappers delegating to statistical-feedback/charts/*.
open_questions: Box/whisker fidelity: fillout_cohort_stats returns n/mean/std_dev/min/max/median/p90/p95/p99 but no p25/p75 — a true box plot needs quartiles. Extend the SQL function (cheap, same percentile_cont call) or accept a mean±SD 'box' approximation as FeedbackChart's 'box' does today?

Dataset filters vs anonymity: which filters are safe on a public endpoint? A conditionGroup or dateRange filter can slice n below 5 (floor must apply post-filter — confirmed intent), but repeated adjacent-filter queries could difference small groups; does the mechanism need per-declaration server-side validation or a whitelist of filter dimensions?

Aggregate computation cost/freshness: keep on-demand SQL per batch request (fine at current scale, published-gate + one function call each) or precompute into a report_aggregates table on a schedule once questionnaires get large? Affects whether computedAt is request-time or job-time.

Version scope default for cohorts: sessions pin exact (major.minor.patch) but 'this study's cohort' usually spans patch/minor versions — is 'same-major' the right default dataset.versionScope, and should the response-key path guard against major versions that changed response keys?

Radar-with-cohort-overlay declares one aggregate per scale — should the editor offer a bulk 'declare aggregates for all scoring scales' action, and is there a cap on declared aggregates per questionnaire (batch endpoint abuse surface)?

Migration/coexistence: does the report page eventually absorb the in-flow statistical-feedback item's end-of-survey usage (designers today put it as the last display item), and should CompletionScreen's legacy scoreConfigs-only PDF path be deprecated in favor of rendering the PDF from ReportPageConfig?

Localization: interpretive-text templates and widget titles should ride the ADR 0022 settings.translations path like completionMessage does (resolveText kind:'chrome') — needs a slot naming scheme for per-widget text (e.g. report.<widgetId>.template).

Should the last-synced aggregates ALSO be embedded in the by-code definition payload as a bootstrap (so the very first offline-capable cache write seeds values without the second request), with the Dexie table then taking over refresh?

design.architecture_overview: E-FEEDBACK-3 is a designable, participant-facing Report Page stored at `settings.report: ReportPageConfig` inside the questionnaire definition JSONB — the same no-DB-migration precedent as `settings.scoring` (packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts:276-296) — so the page layout version-pins and ships offline inside the Dexie `filloutQuestionnaires` snapshot that `(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.ts` already loads. Server-side aggregates become first-class via a single questionnaire-level registry, `settings.serverAggregates: ServerAggregateDecl[]` (NOT nested under settings.report, resolving the two designs' conflict): one registry, two consumers — Report Page widgets and the existing in-flow statistical-feedback display item both bind by `aggregateId` through one new `'cohort-synced'` source mode added to apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts. On the server, migration 00030 adds `public.fillout_feedback_stats(qid, source, key, filter jsonb)` — the 00028 `fillout_cohort_stats` body generalized with designer-declared dataset-filter predicates and p25/p75 (adopted from the report design so box plots get true quartiles) — exposed by a new anonymous-safe batch endpoint `GET /api/questionnaires/{id}/feedback-aggregates?version=maj.min.patch` that resolves declarations from the PUBLISHED definition server-side (the client never sends a filter) and enforces the existing MIN_COHORT_N=5 floor per aggregate (apps/server/src/api/sessions/models.rs:1361, pattern of public_cohort_stats at analytics.rs:584). The client caches results in a new Dexie version(5) table `filloutAggregates` keyed `[definitionKey+aggregateId]` — the mechanism design's version-pinned keying wins over the report design's questionnaireId keying because a pinned resumed session must read values computed for ITS declarations — with the report design's `declHash` field added per row to allow safe best-effort cross-version fallback when the declaration is byte-identical. Values still evolve as data accrues (the report design's valid concern) via three refresh points: an awaited-with-3s-cap fetch in the +page.ts online branch immediately after `FilloutContentCache.cacheQuestionnaire` (+page.ts:64), a `?version=` fetch when a resume resolves a pinned older version, and an opportunistic refresh after a successful `FilloutUploadSync.syncNow()` drain. At fillout page init, one Dexie read builds an in-memory `Record<aggregateId, SyncedAggregate>` handed into the runtime context; from then on every render is synchronous and network-free — unlike today's `'cohort'`/`'participant-vs-cohort'` modes which call `sessionAnalyticsService.aggregatePublic` live (engine.ts:481) and break offline. Rendering is the report design's hybrid: a new `ReportPageView.svelte` composes typed widgets on a 12-column grid whose WidgetPosition shape is COPIED from analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts:14-23 (not imported — analytics widgets are authenticated, online-only researcher tools), while the data/chart layer REUSES the statistical-feedback engine, its charts directory, ScoreInterpreter bands, and ReportGenerator PDF. CompletionScreen finally gets its dead wires fixed: `completedSession.variables` (populated — QuestionnaireRuntime.complete() runs applyScaleScores() at QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1636) and `reportConfig` are passed at the +page.svelte:889 mount site, and ReportPageView mounts inside CompletionScreen as plain Svelte (the completion screen sits outside the runtime item loop, so no FormQuestionHost). Fallbacks are layered and honest: synced row with n>=5 → n<5 count-only message → designer-opted bundled norm table (explicitly labeled "published norms, not this study") → participant-only rendering with a reconnect hint. The north-star demo works offline by construction: box widget = mean±sd/p25/median/p75 from the cached row, participant marker = `score.anxiety.value` from the VariableEngine (E-FEEDBACK-1 ScaleScorer output), caption "n=142, cohort data as of 2026-07-04".
design.offline_data_flow: DECLARE (design time): the researcher adds a box-cohort widget in ReportPageEditor and binds it to `score.anxiety.value` with comparison 'cohort-synced'; the editor creates (or reuses, deduped by declHash) a declaration in `settings.serverAggregates`: `{ id: 'agg_anxiety_f', source: 'variable', key: 'score.anxiety.value', stats: ['n','mean','sd','min','max','p25','median','p75'], dataset: { id: 'females', versionScope: 'sameMajor', where: [{ var: 'demo.gender', op: 'eq', value: 'f' }] } }`; the widget stores only `aggregateId: 'agg_anxiety_f'` + `fallback: 'norm-table'` + `fallbackNormTableId`. Publishing bumps minor and the registry rides the definition JSONB. ONLINE SYNC/CACHE (first participant visit): the online branch of routes/(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.ts caches the definition (`FilloutContentCache.cacheQuestionnaire`, line 64), then awaits new `FilloutContentCache.cacheAggregates(payload)` with a ~3s Promise.race cap (failure → catch → proceed; never blocks load): it GETs `/api/questionnaires/{id}/feedback-aggregates?version=maj.min.patch`; the SERVER checks the published gate (status='published' AND deleted_at IS NULL, 404 otherwise), loads the definition JSONB for that version (questionnaire_snapshots from 00010 for pinned older versions), reads `settings.serverAggregates` (cap 50), runs `fillout_feedback_stats` per declaration on &state.pool (SECURITY DEFINER crosses RLS, aggregates-only containment per 00028's documented argument), applies MIN_COHORT_N=5 per aggregate post-filter (n<5 → count kept, stats null), and answers `{ questionnaire_id, version, computed_at, aggregates: [...] }` from a Redis TTL cache (key feedagg:{qid}:{ver}, ~300s) so anonymous spikes don't fan out. The client writes each row to Dexie `filloutAggregates` as `{ definitionKey: filloutDefinitionKey(qid,maj,min,patch), aggregateId, declHash, questionnaireId, source, key, datasetId, n, stats|null, computedAt, syncedAt }`. Resumed pinned sessions (+page.ts pin branch, line 136-180) fetch with `?version=pin` so pinned declarations get fresh values under their own definitionKey; a successful FilloutUploadSync drain triggers an opportunistic refresh (the cohort that just absorbed this participant updates promptly). OFFLINE FILLOUT READS CACHE: at +page.svelte runtime construction, once definitionKey is known, ONE read — `db.filloutAggregates.where('definitionKey').equals(key).toArray()` — builds `Record<aggregateId, SyncedAggregate>` passed alongside variables into the runtime context; if a widget's aggregateId has no row for the exact definitionKey, a secondary lookup accepts a row from another definitionKey of the same questionnaireId ONLY when declHash matches (byte-identical declaration → semantically safe). WIDGET RENDERS (zero network): participant completes → applyScaleScores writes `score.anxiety` {value,z,tScore,percentile,band} into the VariableEngine (persisted via OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable, FilloutRuntime.ts handleComplete); +page.svelte passes completedVariables + reportConfig + the aggregates snapshot to CompletionScreen → ReportPageView; the engine's 'cohort-synced' branch resolves the participant value via the existing resolveVariableValue dotted-path walker (engine.ts:104) and maps the cached stats into buildParticipantVsCohortSeriesLocal (sessionAnalytics.ts:234) → same ChartSeriesContract → FeedbackChart chartType 'box' draws box (p25/median/p75), whiskers (min/max), mean±sd band, and the participant marker, captioned "n=142, as of <computedAt>". No fetch() exists anywhere on this path. FALLBACKS in order: (1) row present n>=5 → normal render + as-of caption; (2) row present n<5 → participant-only + "Group comparison needs at least 5 completed responses (currently n)"; (3) no row (fetch failed then device offline) → if fallbackNormTableId, degrade to the E-FEEDBACK-2 bundled norm path (runtime/feedback/normTables.ts) labeled "compared against published norms, not this study's participants"; else per-widget fallback 'hide' or "Group comparison unavailable offline — reconnect to see how you compare"; (4) stale row (> staleAfterMs, default 30d) → still render (offline correctness beats freshness) + visible staleness note; (5) pinnedFallback miss (+page.ts:180) → declHash-matched cross-version row or case 3.
data_model: packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts — QuestionnaireSettings gains `serverAggregates?: ServerAggregateDecl[]` and `report?: ReportPageConfig` (definition-JSON only, no relational change; precedent settings.scoring at :293-296). ServerAggregateDecl { id, source: 'variable'|'response', key, stats: Array<'n'|'mean'|'sd'|'min'|'max'|'p25'|'median'|'p75'|'p90'|'p95'|'p99'>, dataset?: DatasetFilter }; DatasetFilter { id, label?, versionScope?: 'any'|'sameMajor'|'exact' (default 'sameMajor'), completedAfter?, completedBefore?, where?: Array<{ var, op: 'eq'|'ne'|'lt'|'lte'|'gt'|'gte'|'in', value }> } — where-clauses restricted to session variables in v1 (session_variable_index from 00012 makes them indexed). Registry capped at 50 at validation time.

ReportPageConfig { enabled, title?, layout: { columns: 12, rowHeight, gap }, widgets: ReportWidget[], enablePdfDownload?, refreshMs?, staleAfterMs? }; ReportWidget { id, type: ReportWidgetType, position: WidgetPosition (shape copied from apps/web/src/lib/analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts:14-23), title?, binding: { source: 'variable'|'score', key, field?: 'value'|'tScore'|'percentile'|'z'|'stanine' }, comparison?: { source: 'none'|'norm-table'|'custom-norm'|'self-baseline'|'cohort-synced', normTableId?, customNorm?, baselineVariable?, aggregateId?, fallback: 'hide'|'norm-table'|'message', fallbackNormTableId? }, interpretation?: ScoreInterpreterConfig, text?: { template, bandVariants? } }. ReportWidgetType = 'score-tile'|'bar'|'box-cohort'|'radar-profile'|'distribution-with-marker'|'gauge'|'trajectory-line'|'interpretive-text'|'results-table'|'completion-meta'.

apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts — StatisticalSourceMode (line 20) gains 'cohort-synced'; StatisticalFeedbackDataSourceConfig gains `aggregateId?: string` and `fallbackNormTableId?: string`; validateStatisticalFeedbackConfig gains a cohort-synced branch (requires currentVariable + aggregateId). Consistent naming: `aggregateId` everywhere (the report design's `aggregateRef` is dropped).

apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts — Dexie version(5) (v4 exists at line 253) adds `filloutAggregates: '[definitionKey+aggregateId], definitionKey, questionnaireId, syncedAt'`; row { definitionKey /* filloutDefinitionKey(qid,maj,min,patch), same composite as filloutQuestionnaires */, aggregateId, declHash /* stable hash of the canonicalized declaration, enables safe cross-version fallback */, questionnaireId, source, key, datasetId?, n, stats: { mean, stdDev, min, max, p25, median, p75, p90, p95, p99 } | null, computedAt /* server clock */, syncedAt /* client clock */ }. Purely additive; all v4 stores restated unchanged.

apps/web/src/lib/runtime/feedback/serverAggregates.ts (new) — collectServerAggregates(definition): deduped declaration list + referential integrity check (every widget/item aggregateId resolves); declHash(decl) canonical hash; SyncedAggregate type shared by FilloutContentCache, page init, and engine.

apps/server/src/api/sessions/models.rs — FeedbackAggregatesResponse { questionnaire_id, version, computed_at, aggregates: Vec<FeedbackAggregateEntry> }; FeedbackAggregateEntry { id, source, key, dataset_id, sample_count, stats: Option<NumericStatsSummary + p25/p75> } (utoipa-annotated; reuses MIN_COHORT_N at :1361).
server_changes: apps/server/migrations/00030_feedback_aggregates.sql — CREATE FUNCTION public.fillout_feedback_stats(qid uuid, p_source text, p_key text, p_filter jsonb) RETURNS TABLE(n bigint, mean float8, std_dev float8, min float8, max float8, p25 float8, median float8, p75 float8, p90 float8, p95 float8, p99 float8): the 00028 vals CTE + JSONB numeric coercion verbatim, plus p25/p75 percentile_cont, plus filter predicates on sessions — versionScope ('sameMajor'/'exact' against s.questionnaire_version_major/minor/patch), completedAfter/Before bounds on s.completed_at, and per where-clause an EXISTS subquery over session_variable_index (00012). Same posture as 00028: LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_temp, OWNER qdesigner, REVOKE FROM PUBLIC, GRANT EXECUTE TO qdesigner_app. fillout_cohort_stats stays untouched (back-compat for live 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' modes).

apps/server/src/api/sessions/analytics.rs — new handler public_feedback_aggregates: (1) published gate exactly as public_cohort_stats (:584) — status='published' AND deleted_at IS NULL, 404 otherwise, no existence probing; (2) resolve definition JSONB — questionnaire_definitions for latest, questionnaire_snapshots (00010) when ?version=maj.min.patch targets a pinned version (fall back to latest registry with a response flag if the snapshot is gone); (3) read settings.serverAggregates — the authorization model: only designer-published filters are evaluable, the anonymous client sends zero filter data (kills crafted-filter differencing probes); (4) run fillout_feedback_stats per declaration (cap 50) on &state.pool — no RLS GUC needed, definer bypasses, as already documented in analytics.rs; (5) MIN_COHORT_N=5 per aggregate post-filter: n<5 → sample_count populated, stats None; (6) respond with computed_at.

apps/server/src/api/mod.rs — .route("/{id}/feedback-aggregates", get(sessions::public_feedback_aggregates)) beside the cohort-stats route at line 180, on the same public/anonymous router (inherits the existing rate-limit middleware).

Redis TTL result cache via state.redis — key feedagg:{qid}:{version}, TTL ~300s, so anonymous load spikes cost one aggregate fan-out per 5 minutes; in-memory fallback mirrors the rate-limiter pattern. Revisit as a materialized table only if cohort size makes 50 scans/5min the bottleneck.
openapi.rs — register the new path + schemas so @qdesigner/contracts regenerates.
client_sync_changes: apps/web/src/lib/fillout/services/FilloutContentCache.ts — new static cacheAggregates(payload, version?): GET /api/questionnaires/{id}/feedback-aggregates?version=…, compute declHash per declaration via collectServerAggregates, bulkPut rows keyed [definitionKey+aggregateId]; skip network when the newest row for the definitionKey is younger than settings.report.refreshMs (default 24h). pruneDefinitions() (:141) gains one clause: delete filloutAggregates rows whose definitionKey was just GC'd (pinned keys at :251-271 remain protected).

routes/(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.ts — online branch (line 64, after cacheQuestionnaire): `await Promise.race([FilloutContentCache.cacheAggregates(payload), timeout(3000)]).catch(() => {})` — lands values before the participant can reach the report on first visit, never blocks load; pin branch (line 136-180): when a resume resolves a pinned older version online, fire cacheAggregates with ?version=pin so the pinned definitionKey gets fresh rows.

apps/web/src/lib/fillout/services/FilloutUploadSync.ts — after a successful syncNow() drain, fire-and-forget cacheAggregates refresh for the active definitionKey (the cohort that just absorbed this participant's completed session updates promptly for the next run on this device). Default-on; uploads unchanged.
apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts — Dexie version(5) with the filloutAggregates store (additive, no .upgrade() body, same pattern as the v3→v4 bump at line 253).

routes/(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte — at runtime construction, one Dexie read builds the in-memory Record<aggregateId, SyncedAggregate> (with the declHash cross-version fallback lookup) and threads it into the runtime context alongside variables/urlParams; at the CompletionScreen mount (:889) pass `variables={Object.fromEntries(completedSession.variables.map(v => [v.variableId, v.value]))}`, `reportConfig={rawDefinition.settings?.report}`, and the aggregates snapshot.
design.designer_ux: A new "Report" panel in the designer, sibling of the existing settings editors (ScaleScoringEditor at lib/components/designer/scoring/, QuotaPanel/DataQualityPanel at lib/components/designer/), hosting ReportPageEditor.svelte: the DashboardBuilder interaction pattern (Add Widget palette sidebar, 12-col grid, WidgetContainer resize/remove) instantiated over the report-specific palette — the shapes are copied from analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts, not imported, because the analytics WidgetType union (:6-12) and its authenticated fetch paths are the wrong stack for a participant runtime. Per-widget config popovers reuse the binding UX from StatisticalFeedbackDesigner.svelte: a score/variable picker offering `score.<scaleId>` entries derived from settings.scoring.scales plus VariableManager variables, a metric-field selector (value/tScore/percentile), norm-table dropdown incl. CUSTOM_NORM_TABLE_ID, and ScoreInterpreterConfig range editors. Choosing comparison 'cohort-synced' opens a create-or-link flow against the shared `settings.serverAggregates` registry: the editor derives the declaration from the widget binding (score.<id> keys work because scores persist as session_variables, which fillout_feedback_stats aggregates by source='variable'), dedupes identical declarations by declHash, and on save garbage-collects declarations unreferenced by EITHER surface — report widgets AND in-flow statistical-feedback items' dataSource.aggregateId (critical GC rule; the registry serves both). A dataset-filter sub-editor covers versionScope (default 'sameMajor'), date bounds, and session-variable where-clauses, with two designer-time lints: (a) a differencing warning when declared datasets are set-complements of each other (e.g. 'all' minus 'female' can expose a small subgroup), and (b) an n-floor reminder that narrow filters will render as "not enough responses yet" until n>=5. A bulk action "declare cohort aggregates for all scoring scales" feeds radar-profile widgets. Preview uses ReportPageView with mode 'preview' (FeedbackRenderMode split already in engine.ts:358): authenticated designers fetch the endpoint live to see real numbers, bypassing the Dexie cache. Everything persists under settings.report/settings.serverAggregates, riding the existing settings persistence, Yjs collaboration, and semver flow (report/aggregate changes = minor bump); widget titles and interpretive-text templates get translation slots `report.<widgetId>.title` / `.template` via the ADR 0022 resolveText path, like completionMessage.
design.runtime_render: One engine, two surfaces. The single new code path is a 'cohort-synced' branch in resolveStatisticalFeedbackSeries (apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts, sibling of the fully-local 'norm-table' branch at :384): participant value = resolveVariableValue(dataSource.currentVariable) (:104 — already walks the namespaced E-FEEDBACK-1 `score.<id>` objects), cohort = syncedAggregates[dataSource.aggregateId] from the page-init snapshot — a pure synchronous lookup, no fetch, unlike the live 'cohort' branch at :481. The cached stats map into sessionAnalyticsService.buildParticipantVsCohortSeriesLocal (apps/web/src/lib/services/sessionAnalytics.ts:234) so z-score math and the ChartSeriesContract are identical, and every existing chart (FeedbackChart box/bar/radar, BellCurveChart, GaugeChart in modules/display/statistical-feedback/charts/) renders unchanged — the box-cohort widget gains true quartiles from the new p25/p75. Surface 1, the Report Page: ReportPageView.svelte (new, lib/fillout/components/) renders the settings.report grid; each widget is a thin wrapper that builds a StatisticalFeedbackDataSourceConfig from its binding and delegates to the engine + charts; score-tile/results-table/completion-meta read ScaleScoreResult fields directly from variables; interpretive-text interpolates {{score.x.tScore}} via resolveVariableValue with per-band variants via ScoreInterpretationRange. It mounts inside CompletionScreen.svelte between the thank-you header and the completion-code block — plain Svelte, NOT via FormQuestionHost/moduleConfigAdapter, because currentScreen==='complete' replaces the canvas+overlay entirely; this also revives the dormant PDF path (showReportButton/handleDownloadReport at CompletionScreen.svelte:140-158) by feeding scoreConfigs from settings.report and extending ReportGenerator to accept synced aggregates. Surface 2, the in-flow statistical-feedback display item keeps its FormQuestionHost presentation (F062 no-auto-dismiss) for mid-survey feedback and gains the same 'cohort-synced' mode for free. Fallback rendering is per-widget and honest (the F060 lesson — never a silent null point): n<5 count message, labeled norm-table degradation, offline-reconnect hint, staleness caption from computedAt.
design.migration: Two migrations, both additive and zero-risk to existing data. (1) SQL: apps/server/migrations/00030_feedback_aggregates.sql (verified: 00029 is the current head) creates ONLY the new SECURITY DEFINER function fillout_feedback_stats — no table, no RLS change, no backfill; 00028's fillout_cohort_stats is untouched so the live 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' modes keep working. Runs automatically via sqlx::migrate! on the qdesigner migration pool at startup. (2) Dexie: version(5) in apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts (verified: version(4) is current, at line 253) adding filloutAggregates — Dexie creates it empty, no .upgrade() body, all v4 stores restated unchanged, no primary-key changes to existing stores. Coexistence: definitions without settings.serverAggregates yield an empty aggregates response and no fetch cost; no widget uses 'cohort-synced' until a designer configures one; existing statistical-feedback items, CompletionScreen behavior, and the cohort-stats endpoint are unchanged. Rollback = drop the function / ignore the empty table.

implementation_steps.detail: Add ServerAggregateDecl, DatasetFilter, ReportPageConfig, ReportWidget, WidgetPosition (copied shape) to questionnaire-core; add serverAggregates and report to QuestionnaireSettings. Create serverAggregates.ts with collectServerAggregates(definition) (dedupe + referential-integrity + cap-50 validation), declHash, and the SyncedAggregate type.

Write 00030_feedback_aggregates.sql: fillout_feedback_stats(qid, source, key, filter jsonb) = 00028 body + p25/p75 percentile_cont + versionScope/date/where-EXISTS predicates over session_variable_index; SECURITY DEFINER, owner qdesigner, REVOKE PUBLIC, GRANT EXECUTE TO qdesigner_app.

Add FeedbackAggregatesResponse/FeedbackAggregateEntry models (+p25/p75 on the stats shape); implement public_feedback_aggregates (published gate, ?version snapshot resolution via 00010, registry read from definition JSONB, per-aggregate function call + MIN_COHORT_N floor, Redis TTL cache feedagg:{qid}:{version}); register the route beside line 180 and in openapi.rs; regenerate contracts.

New tests: filter predicates (versionScope/date/where) select the right sessions; n<5 post-filter returns count-only null stats; unpublished questionnaire 404s; declaration cap enforced; ?version resolves the snapshot registry. Use the DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS fixture-seeding pattern from tests/rls_enforcement.rs.

Add filloutAggregates ('[definitionKey+aggregateId], definitionKey, questionnaireId, syncedAt') as version(5); implement FilloutContentCache.cacheAggregates (fetch, declHash, bulkPut, refreshMs skip) and extend pruneDefinitions to GC aggregate rows; wire the awaited-3s-cap call into the +page.ts online branch and the ?version=pin fetch into the pin branch; add the post-drain refresh to FilloutUploadSync.

Add the mode to StatisticalSourceMode, aggregateId/fallbackNormTableId to the data-source config, the validation branch, and the resolve branch: snapshot lookup → map stats into buildParticipantVsCohortSeriesLocal → ChartSeriesContract; implement the layered fallback ladder (n<5 message, norm-table degradation with label, offline message, staleness caption). Vitest units against a fake snapshot — no network in any test.

At +page.svelte runtime construction, load the definitionKey's aggregate rows in one Dexie read (with declHash cross-version fallback), build Record<aggregateId, SyncedAggregate>, and thread it to where the engine resolves series (mirroring how urlParams/variables flow).

Build the runtime grid renderer and the ten widget wrappers (score-tile, bar, box-cohort, radar-profile, distribution-with-marker, gauge, trajectory-line, interpretive-text, results-table, completion-meta) as thin delegations onto engine.ts + charts/ + ScoreInterpreter; localize titles/templates via ADR 0022 resolveText slots report.<widgetId>.*.

Fix the dead wires at +page.svelte:889: pass completedVariables (from completedSession.variables) and reportConfig (rawDefinition.settings?.report); CompletionScreen gains reportConfig prop and mounts ReportPageView above the completion-code block; feed scoreConfigs from settings.report so the existing PDF button (:140-158) comes alive; extend ReportGenerator to accept synced aggregates.

New Report panel hosting ReportPageEditor (copied grid/palette pattern, report palette); per-widget config popovers reusing StatisticalFeedbackDesigner binding UX; cohort-synced create-or-link flow against settings.serverAggregates with declHash dedupe, dual-surface GC (report widgets + in-flow items), differencing + n-floor lints, bulk declare-for-all-scales action; add the cohort-synced option to StatisticalFeedbackDesigner for the in-flow item; preview mode fetches live.

Run the full phase gate (pnpm check/test/build, cargo check/test --include-ignored) and the live browser demo per the test plan — the offline box-chart-vs-cohort golden path is the acceptance criterion (live QA is a required gate in this repo).

risks: Differencing across designer-declared aggregates: 'all' minus 'female' can reveal a subgroup below the floor by construction. Mitigation shipped: designer-time complement lint + the fact that only published, finite declarations are computable (no caller-supplied filters). Open hardening: server-side floor on pairwise complements — deferred, documented in the ADR.

Snapshot retention for ?version resolution: a pinned older session's declarations resolve from questionnaire_snapshots (00010); if the snapshot is gone the server falls back to the latest registry, where an aggregateId may have changed meaning — declHash on the client rejects semantically-drifted rows, but the fetch then yields nothing for that id (fallback ladder case 3). Acceptable, but document.

First-load latency: the awaited 3s-capped aggregates fetch adds up to 3s to the worst-case first paint of /q/[code]. Bounded and catch-wrapped, but monitor; can be demoted to fire-and-forget if completion-time cache-arrival proves reliable in practice.

Cost of up-to-50 fillout_feedback_stats calls per uncached request: the Redis TTL cache (300s) bounds it, but where-EXISTS predicates over session_variable_index on large cohorts need an EXPLAIN pass; a materialized aggregate table refreshed on completion is the escape hatch.

versionScope 'sameMajor' default can mix minor-version cohorts whose response keys changed only under a major bump — correct per the semver table (response-key changes = major), but response-source aggregates on freshly-majored questionnaires will show n<5 until the new cohort accrues.

Dual-surface registry GC: the report editor must not GC a declaration still referenced by an in-flow statistical-feedback item (and vice versa) — collectServerAggregates' referential-integrity check must scan both, or a live widget silently loses its aggregate.

CompletionScreen mounts outside the runtime item loop: ReportPageView must not assume FormQuestionHost context (theme tokens, i18n message context must be reachable from CompletionScreen's tree); verify in live QA, not just types.

Dexie v5 bump: additive, but any concurrently-open old tab holds a v4 connection — Dexie handles versionchange, yet the fillout page should tolerate a blocked upgrade gracefully (existing pattern from v3→v4 applies).
Clock skew: staleness uses client syncedAt and server computedAt; captions should prefer computedAt (server clock) to avoid nonsense 'as of' dates on skewed devices.
test_plan: Server integration (apps/server/tests/feedback_aggregates.rs): seed sessions + session_variable_index under the migrations DSN; assert fillout_feedback_stats filter predicates (versionScope sameMajor/exact, date bounds, where eq/in on session variables) select exactly the right sessions; per-aggregate n<5 → sample_count set, stats null; unpublished/deleted questionnaire → 404 with no existence leak; cap 50 enforced; ?version resolves the snapshot registry; p25/median/p75 match percentile_cont hand-computed values.

SQL security posture test: as qdesigner_app inside a tx with no GUCs set, EXECUTE fillout_feedback_stats succeeds and returns only aggregates; direct SELECT on sessions for the same rows is RLS-denied (mirrors the 00028 containment argument).

Engine unit tests (vitest, apps/web): 'cohort-synced' branch resolves participant value from a score.<id> object via the dotted-path walker; maps cached stats through buildParticipantVsCohortSeriesLocal to the exact ChartSeriesContract of the live 'cohort' mode; fallback ladder — n<5 message, fallbackNormTableId degradation with the 'published norms' label, no-row offline message, staleAfterMs caption; validateStatisticalFeedbackConfig rejects cohort-synced without aggregateId. Zero network in all tests.

Dexie tests: version(5) upgrade from a seeded v4 DB preserves all stores; [definitionKey+aggregateId] roundtrip; pruneDefinitions GCs aggregate rows with their definitionKey but never pinned keys; declHash cross-version fallback returns a row only on hash match.
collectServerAggregates unit tests: dedupe by declHash, cap 50, referential integrity across BOTH report widgets and in-flow items, GC-safety (unreferenced-by-both only).

THE OFFLINE BOX-CHART-VS-COHORT DEMO (acceptance criterion, live browser per the repo's live-QA gate): (1) seed >=5 completed sessions with score.anxiety values via the fillout flow; (2) designer: add a box-cohort widget bound to score.anxiety.value with a cohort-synced aggregate, publish; (3) open /q/[code] online once — verify Network shows one feedback-aggregates call and Dexie filloutAggregates has the row; (4) DevTools → Network → Offline; (5) complete the survey fully offline; (6) CompletionScreen renders the ReportPageView box chart: box from cached p25/median/p75, mean±sd band, participant marker from the just-computed score, caption 'n=X, as of <computedAt>' — with the Network tab proving zero requests during fillout and render; (7) reload offline and resume → same render (pinned definitionKey rows).

Fallback QA offline: fresh profile, block the aggregates request (but not the definition), go offline, complete → widget shows the norm-table degradation label or the reconnect message per widget fallback; cohort with n=3 → 'needs at least 5 responses (currently 3)'.

Version-pin QA: start a session, publish a minor bump with a changed declaration, resume the old session offline → it reads its own definitionKey rows (or declHash-matched fallback), never the new declaration's values.
In-flow parity: a mid-survey statistical-feedback item with sourceMode 'cohort-synced' renders offline identically to the report widget (one engine, two surfaces).

Regression gate: full baseline — pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check/test/build, scripting-engine test, cargo check/test --include-ignored — plus existing cohort-stats endpoint tests untouched (00028 path unchanged).
